title: Staffing Impact, Applicant Tracking Gap, Concierge Onboarding, AS400 Sunset Timeline, and Regional Systems Landscape
summary: Staff deployment question: new system won't reduce headcount but redirects effort from manual compilation to value-added analytics (e.g., analyzing water department overtime spikes vs just processing paychecks). Current applicant tracking entirely paper-based; city lost Indeed full suite access -- limited to 3 job postings/month, no automated applicant communications. UKG applicant tracking module provides centralized database, automated emails to applicants, concierge onboarding (pre-hire paperwork, integrated training, E-Verify). AS400 sunset targets: timekeeping decommissioned July 1, 2026 (successful SWC rollout); payroll/HR demographics July 1, 2027 (12-18 month UKG Pro implementation). AS400 also used for water billing and assessment -- those remain. County landscape: PeopleSoft for general ledger/AP/AR (city piggybacks); county invested heavily in PeopleSoft project costing. City grant cost allocation currently 100% manual journal entries -- modern system would automate grant reimbursement tracking (e.g., police overtime auto-allocated to grant). Rochester school district spent $30M+ on Oracle. No New York municipality currently live on UKG Pro; State of Indiana and Boulder, Colorado cited. Council asks about city peer models.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on AS400 sunset timeline and asking for peer city models as validation

processing_notes: HR and IT in detected_departments are contextually correct; API, HR, Finance are presenting departments
Indeed = job posting platform (city lost full suite access -- significant hiring disadvantage)
E-Verify = federal employment eligibility verification; PeopleSoft = Oracle's older HR/ERP used by county
Rochester school district $30M+ Oracle implementation = regional cautionary tale
July 1 2026 AS400 timekeeping sunset and July 1 2027 payroll sunset = key milestones to track
Segment 5 of 7 from Finance Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 3, 2026
